## AbstractModelClass.updateOne() method

Updates a single document in the database that matches the specified filter with the provided update payload.

**Signature:**

```typescript
static updateOne(filter: QueryRootFilter, payload: QueryExecutorUpdateManyUpdater<DocumentType>, options?: QueryUpdateOneOptions): Query<any, any>;
```

## Parameters

<table><thead><tr><th>

Parameter


</th><th>

Type


</th><th>

Description


</th></tr></thead>
<tbody><tr><td>

filter


</td><td>

QueryRootFilter


</td><td>

The filter object to specify the search criteria for the document to be updated.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

payload


</td><td>

QueryExecutorUpdateManyUpdater&lt;DocumentType&gt;


</td><td>

The update payload specifying the fields to be updated and their new values or a update function.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

options


</td><td>

QueryUpdateOneOptions


</td><td>

_(Optional)_ Optional settings for the updateOne operation.


</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

**Returns:**

Query&lt;any, any&gt;

A promise that resolves with the result of the update operation, including information about whether a document was matched and modified.

## Remarks

When using updateOne, the values will not be validated against the schema. If the document need to validate, use `schema.validate` method before calling updateOne.

## Example


```ts
const result = await UserModel.updateOne(
  { _id: '123' },
  { $set: { name: 'John Doe' } }
);
console.log(result);
```
